text:
Searching for papers on neural coupling... 🔍

The generic "neural coupling" query returned mostly unrelated results (neural networks, photonics). Let me refine with neuroscience-specific terms.

Here are **10 recent papers on neural coupling** from PubMed (2025–2026):

---

## 📚 Neural Coupling Papers

### 1. Prefrontal Speaker-Listener Neural Coupling Supports Speech-in-Noise Comprehension in Normal-Hearing Older Adults
- **Authors:** Li Z, Liu Y, Zhang X, Kou N, Zhao X, Jia X, Engel AK, Zhang D, Wang S
- **Journal:** *European Journal of Neuroscience* (2026)
- **DOI:** [10.1111/ejn.70392](https://doi.org/10.1111/ejn.70392)
- **TLDR:** fNIRS hyperscanning study showing prefrontal speaker-listener neural coupling supports speech-in-noise comprehension in older adults. Directly relevant to your research area! 🎯

### 2. Communication Modality Modulates Dynamic Switching Between Neural Coupling and Decoupling During Group Creative Ideation
- **Authors:** Cheng S, Zhang W, Xie C, Hao N
- **Journal:** *NeuroImage* (2026)
- **DOI:** [10.1016/j.neuroimage.2026.121707](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.neuroimage.2026.121707)
- **TLDR:** Examines how communication modality affects dynamic switching between neural coupling and decoupling during group creativity tasks.

### 3. Hierarchical Neurobehavioral Model Reveals That Shared Flexibility, Not Individual Stability, Supports Rhythmic Coordination
- **Authors:** Niu R, Li Y, Liu L, Pan Y, Liu Y
- **Journal:** *NeuroImage* (2026)
- **DOI:** [10.1016/j.neuroimage.2026.121773](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.neuroimage.2026.121773)
- **TLDR:** Hierarchical modeling shows shared flexibility (not individual stability) drives rhythmic interpersonal coordination — relevant to music/speech coupling work.

### 4. Testing the Causal Relationship Between Interpersonal Closeness and Inter-Brain Synchrony
- **Authors:** Fornari L, Janssen TWP, Davidesco I, Krabbendam L, van Atteveldt N
- **Journal:** *Scientific Reports* (2026)
- **DOI:** [10.1038/s41598-026-36958-5](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41598-026-36958-5)
- **TLDR:** Tests whether interpersonal closeness causally drives inter-brain synchrony (not just correlates). Important methodological contribution to the hyperscanning field.

### 5. Cross-Recurrence Quantification Analysis Captures Inter-Brain Coupling During Naturalistic Negotiation
- **Authors:** Goldstein BM, Burns SM, Peck FC, Dale R, Lieberman MD
- **Journal:** *Frontiers in Neuroscience* (2025)
- **DOI:** [10.3389/fnins.2025.1713357](https://doi.org/10.3389/fnins.2025.1713357)
- **TLDR:** Introduces cross-recurrence quantification as a new dynamic approach for measuring inter-brain coupling during naturalistic negotiation via hyperscanning.

### 6. Coupling of Rhythms in Prefrontal Cortex and Autonomic Nervous System in School-Age Children
- **Authors:** Steffen G, Lindig K, Howard CJ, Jerry C, Bell C, Morrow KE, Gallegos D, Oshri A, Suveg C, Kello C, Abney DH
- **Journal:** *Annals of the New York Academy of Sciences* (2026)
- **DOI:** [10.1111/nyas.70203](https://doi.org/10.1111/nyas.70203)
- **TLDR:** Examines prefrontal-autonomic rhythm coupling in children, bridging neural and physiological synchrony.

### 7. Neurobridge: Bridging Functional and Structural Brain Networks via Neural Coupling
- **Authors:** Li Y, Dong R, Liu X, Yuan Y, Kong Y
- **Journal:** *Medical Image Analysis* (2026)
- **DOI:** [10.1016/j.media.2026.103993](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.media.2026.103993)
- **TLDR:** Proposes a consistency-guided dynamic graph learning method to bridge functional and structural brain networks through neural coupling.

### 8. Extracellular Stimulation and Ephaptic Coupling of Neurons (EMI Model)
- **Authors:** Jæger KH, Tveito A
- **Journal:** *Frontiers in Computational Neuroscience* (2026)
- **DOI:** [10.3389/fncom.2026.1755548](https://doi.org/10.3389/fncom.2026.1755548)
- **TLDR:** Finite element modeling of extracellular stimulation and ephaptic (non-synaptic) coupling between neurons.

### 9. Motor Preparation-Execution Coupling in Post-Stroke Gait
- **Authors:** Ito H, Yamaguchi H, Ukai K, Yamauchi R, Kitai K, Kodama T
- **Journal:** *Clinical Neurophysiology Practice* (2026)
- **DOI:** [10.1016/j.cnp.2026.01.004](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cnp.2026.01.004)
- **TLDR:** Pilot study assessing motor preparation-execution neural coupling during ankle dorsiflexion in stroke patients.

### 10. Cognitive Neural Mechanisms of Face-to-Face Interpersonal Synchronous Stepping (fNIRS)
- **Authors:** Li Y, Liu L, Li P, Liu Y
- **Journal:** *Brain and Cognition* (2026)
- **DOI:** [10.1016/j.bandc.2026.106395](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.bandc.2026.106395)
- **TLDR:** fNIRS study on neural mechanisms of interpersonal synchrony during face-to-face coordinated stepping.

---

**Papers #1, #3, and #4** are probably most relevant to your speaker-listener coupling research. Paper #1 in particular (Li et al.) is right in your wheelhouse — prefrontal speaker-listener coupling + speech-in-noise. Want me to dig deeper into any of these?
